reasoning: Myiasis is an infection caused by fly larvae (maggots). Developing vaccines against metazoan parasites is notoriously difficult, and a literature search confirms that there are currently no effective vaccines for myiasis and no promising candidates in late-stage clinical trials. Vaccine development, approval, and subsequent updating of Wikipedia is a process that takes years. Even if an animal or experimental vaccine were suddenly recognized by Wikipedia, the probability of this occurring within the next 6-18 months without prior successful clinical trials is exceptionally low. Given the short time horizons (ranging from 1 week to 6 months out) and the complete absence of any myiasis vaccine close to approval, the probability of the Wikipedia article listing 'Yes' for a Myiasis vaccine by these dates is very close to zero. We assign 2% to the closest date, gradually increasing to 4% for the furthest date to account for random vandalism or unexpected novel veterinary vaccines being added to the article.
